title: XIFT v1.0 Identity Handshake Messages (Channel 1)
description: Request/response bodies for the two-phase identity handshake (Channel 1 §7, cross-channel primitive in channels-general §2). XIFT specifies Phase A (method negotiation) and the OUTPUT of Phase B (session token); Phase B internals are identity-provider-specific and are carried inside the open `initiator_proof` / `responder_proof` / `trust_score_assertion` objects. Each message shape is a named definition under $defs so OpenAPI/AsyncAPI can reference it directly; the root oneOf allows standalone validation of any single handshake message.

handshake_init_request.title: Handshake Init Request (Phase A)
handshake_init_request.description: POST /xift/v1/handshake/init. Initiator proposes the methods it supports.

supported_methods.description: Handshake methods the initiator supports (e.g. "iatp-v1"). No overlap with the responder => protocol:channel1:handshake_method_unsupported (105).

handshake_init_response.title: Handshake Init Response (Phase A)
handshake_init_response.description: Responder selects a mutually supported method and issues a challenge for Phase B.

method.description: The single method selected from the intersection of both parties' supported_methods.

challenge_nonce.description: Base64url challenge the initiator must answer in the complete request (replay protection).

session_token_ttl_seconds.description: TTL the responder intends to apply to the issued session token.

handshake_complete_request.title: Handshake Complete Request (Phase B output)
handshake_complete_request.description: POST /xift/v1/handshake/complete. Carries the method-specific proof answering the challenge, plus an optional trust-score assertion.

initiator_proof.description: Method-specific proof of identity answering challenge_nonce. Opaque to XIFT; shape depends on the selected method.

trust_score_assertion.description: OPTIONAL method-specific assertion of the initiator's trust score (e.g. a verifiable credential or reference). Opaque to XIFT.

handshake_complete_response.title: Handshake Complete Response (Phase B output)
handshake_complete_response.description: Successful completion yields a bearer session token used on all subsequent channel requests (Authorization: Bearer; channels-general §1.4, §2.3).

session_token.description: Bearer token authorizing subsequent Channel 2-7 requests. Invalid/forged => protocol:channel1:session_token_invalid (101); past expiry => protocol:channel1:session_token_expired (105).

responder_trust_score.description: The responder's trust score, disclosed to the initiator. No 700 Custodian gate applies in peer-to-peer Flow A; baseline is 500 (core §10).

responder_proof.description: Method-specific proof of the responder's identity (mutual authentication). Opaque to XIFT.

method_specific.x-xift-note: Intentionally open: contents depend on the selected identity-provider handshake method (Phase B). XIFT does not constrain the internal shape; the protocol layer validates it against the negotiated method.
